title: IaP Provider Conformance Case v1
description: Schema for declarative capability-assertion cases (apiVersion conformance.iap.dev/v1) per IEP-0012. The shared harness runs the package's mapping over the referenced corpus document with the fixed inputs, then evaluates each capability through the package's attestation functions — pure predicates over plan attributes registered per (capability, target type) — and compares the verdict to expect.

case.description: Unique case name within the package's conformance corpus.

document.description: Corpus-relative path of the Conforming Document the mapping is applied to (unless an assertion overrides it).

profile.description: Active profile the document is canonicalized against before mapping; omitted means the base document.

mappingInputs.description: Explicit mapping inputs (named scalar parameters) supplied to the invocation; part of the plan's hashed input identity (ch. 12 §12.2 — never ambient lookups).

assertions.description: Machine-checkable claims evaluated against the generated plan (or against the rejection outcome).

relativePath.description: Corpus-relative POSIX path. Segments MUST NOT be . or .. and the path MUST NOT be absolute.

assertion.description: One capability assertion: which resource, which abstract capability, and the expected verdict.

id.description: Unique assertion identifier within the case.

select.description: Which document resource the assertion is evaluated against; omitted (typical for expect: rejected) evaluates against every resource.

resource.description: Resource identifier in the corpus document.

kind.description: Optional kind guard; the selected resource MUST declare this kind.

capability.description: Abstract capability being asserted, in Chapter 5 vocabulary (e.g. encryption.atRest, exposure.private, availability.zonesMinimum).

params.description: Scalar parameters passed to the attestation function (e.g. min: 2 for availability.zonesMinimum).

expect.description: Expected verdict: satisfied (attestations hold on the plan), rejected (the mapping fails closed with diagnostics), or unsupported (no attestation is registered for the capability on any produced target type).

document.description: Optional per-assertion corpus document override (used by expect: rejected cases exercising out-of-matrix documents).
